title: Remote invite consent reaches customer (dbg137)
description:
After dbg136 wired the admin remote panel, Mike could send a remote connection request and see "waiting for customer to accept", but the customer (Kat) never got the consent popup.

Root cause: remote invites were delivered only via in-memory SSE with no fallback. If the customer's EventSource was not connected yet (support init is deferred on requestIdleCallback), or the SSE event was missed during reconnect, the invite was lost. Admin retries for an existing pending session also returned success without re-broadcasting remote-request.

This release adds a customer-side poll on each presence heartbeat for pending remote invites, re-broadcasts remote-request when an admin retries a pending session, retries SSE wiring when the session token was not ready at first boot, and resolves support user id from id or userId like the backend presence heartbeat does.

Who is affected: Horizon admins and customers using PipeShare/PipeSync remote support on OVH private server. SaaS when Mike applies the platform version in releases.html.
changeLog: Customer poll: pending remote invite shown via GET /saas/support/remote/active on heartbeat (SSE fallback).
Backend re-broadcasts remote-request when admin retries an existing pending invite.
SSE client retries connection when session token was missing at first init.
Support user id resolves from user.id or user.userId for invite matching.
